channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Gaza filmmakers slam BBC after shelved documentary wins Bafta
item.description: 'We refuse to be silenced and censored,' journalist and presenter Ramita Navai says while accepting the award.

item.title: Fans celebrate as Barcelona clinches LaLiga title
item.description: Barcelona fans filled the streets in wild celebration after a convincing 2-0 El Clasico win over Real Madrid.

item.title: UK’s Keir Starmer faces likely challenge following Labour’s election defeat
item.description: The UK prime minister is set to give a speech on Monday in a bid to convince the public of his leadership.

item.title: Power tussle grips Philippine Congress amid move to impeach VP Sara Duterte
item.description: Trial of vice president in question after allies of Duterte stages a coup and wrestles control of the Philippine Senate.

item.title: Timberwolves win, tie playoff series after Spurs’ Wembanyama ejected
item.description: Edwards's 36 points give Minnesota 114-109 win and tie the Western Conference semifinals 2-2 against San Antonio Spurs.

item.title: Things are not going so well for Russia
item.description: Stalled advance in Ukraine and economic woes are not the only worries for the Kremlin - also a distant ‘near abroad'.

item.title: Trump calls Iran response “totally unacceptable”
item.description: Report on the latest developments after US President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to the US peace proposal.

item.title: Risks and rewards in France-Kenya partnership
item.description: Nairobi's partnership with Paris has sparked criticism, with many worried Kenya's interests will be subordinated.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse the other of ceasefire violations
item.description: Russia and Ukraine accuse the other of ceasefire violations.

item.title: Thailand’s Thaksin Shinawatra released from prison
item.description: Influential former prime minister released on parole after spending about eight months behind bars.

item.title: Gaza documentary dropped by the BBC wins BAFTA
item.description: A documentary exposing Israel's attacks on Gaza's health system and personnel won Best Current Affairs at the BAFTAs.

item.title: What next for Real Madrid after Barcelona’s La Liga and Clasico triumph?
item.description: Barca sealed their La Liga title defence in the best way possible on Sunday, but where do Real go now, after their loss?

item.title: Somalis rally against government-ordered evictions in Mogadishu
item.description: Demonstrators rallied across the Somali capital in support of families displaced by a wave of government-led evictions.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US continues to have ‘unreasonable demands’
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson says the United States continues to have “unreasonable demands”

item.title: Barcelona beat Real Madrid 2-0 in El Clasico to retain La Liga title
item.description: Barcelona secure 29th Spanish league title after beating Madrid, with goals from Marcus Rashford and Ferran Torres.

item.title: Trump to discuss Iran with Xi Jinping during China visit: Officials
item.description: US president will likely 'apply pressure' to China for buying Iranian oil during war.

item.title: Israeli weapon fires tiny metal cubes into people in Lebanon, like Gaza
item.description: Tiny tungsten cubes from Israeli bombs are causing devastating internal injuries to people in Lebanon.

item.title: US Jewish leader, Israel advocate Abe Foxman dies at 86
item.description: Israeli officials hail Foxman, who led the ADL advocacy group for nearly three decades, as warm and passionate.

item.title: Indian film star Vijay becomes chief minister of Tamil Nadu
item.description: Indian actor C Joseph Vijay has been sworn in as Tamil Nadu’s chief minister after his TVK Party won in elections.

item.title: South Korea says ship was struck by ‘unidentified flying objects’ in Hormuz
item.description: South Korea says it is investigating an incident on May 4 when two unidentified objects struck a Korean-operated cargo s

item.title: Trossard scores late as Arsenal beats West Ham, moves closer to title
item.description: Winger Leandro Trossard scores the match's only goal as Arsenal survives VAR controversy to win at West Ham.

item.title: Gaza volunteers plant olive trees at devastated al-Shifa Hospital
item.description: Volunteers at al-Shifa Hospital cleared rubble and planted olive trees in the courtyard as a symbol of hope.

item.title: Medics among 51 killed in Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Israeli attacks are intensifying in southern Lebanon, with 552 killed since the 'ceasefire' started on April 16.

item.title: Iran may ‘give assurances on the use of nuclear facilities’
item.description: Iran may negotiate on nuclear facilities, but it 'won't destroy uranium or allow it to be moved'

item.title: Trump says US will not allow Iran to reach enriched uranium
item.description: US president says Washington has the nuclear material in Iran 'surveilled' and will 'blow up' anyone who gets near it.
